citationVerifications.sourceSaysVsClaim: Stage-2 re-fetched leginfo on 2026-06-24. Live text confirms §39800(a) discretionary transport authority and §39800(b) 'municipally owned transit system' definition cross-referencing Pub. Util. Code §24501 (Part 1 of Div. 10). Stats. 1999, Ch. 646, eff. 1/1/2000. Matches Stage 1.

Stage-2 re-fetched live text verbatim: $10,000 expenditure trigger, 'secure bids pursuant to Sections 20111 and 20112 of the Public Contract Code,' common-carrier / municipally owned transit / parent-or-guardian exemptions, and 'may let the contract for the service to other than the lowest bidder.' Stats. 1999, Ch. 646. Matches the Rebid-field claim verbatim. Load-bearing citation re-verified.

Stage-2 re-fetched live text verbatim: (a) 'a term not to exceed five years' for continuing pupil-transportation services contract; renewable 'at the option of the school district and the party contracting to provide transportation services, jointly,' with renewed contract carrying forward 'all of the terms and conditions of the previous contract, including any provisions increasing rates based on increased costs'; (b) 5-year lease/rental cap with 10-year variant if district may annually purchase or cancel; (c) annual in-term renegotiation when economic factors require; (d) §17450 et seq. lease cross-reference. Stats. 1999, Ch. 646. Matches Max-Term and Renewal claims verbatim. Load-bearing citation re-verified.

Stage-2 re-fetched live text verbatim: 15-year cap on zero-emission continuing services contract; 15-year zero-emission lease/rental cap with 20-year variant if LEA may annually purchase or cancel; 'local educational agency' = school district, county office of education, or charter school. Stats. 2023, Ch. 445 (AB 579), eff. 1/1/2024. Matches zero-emission carve-out claims verbatim. Load-bearing citation re-verified.

Stage-2 re-fetched live text: $50,000 statutory base threshold for purchases/services; lowest-responsible-bidder rule confirmed; subdivision (e) annual Implicit Price Deflator adjustment confirmed. Last amended Stats. 2017, Ch. 395 (SB 544), eff. 1/1/2018. Per CDE Bid Threshold Letter dated 12/8/2025 (re-fetched, see below), the indexed services threshold = $119,100 eff. 1/1/2026 (3.772% increase from $114,800). The '$119,100 effective 1/1/2026' figure is the CDE-issued INDEXED value per (e), not statutory text — ledger Currency cell conflates the statutory amendment date and the indexed-figure date (P3 hygiene).

Stage-2 spot-checked (not in Stage-1 citationVerifications but cited by §39802). Live text confirms publication / two-weeks-of-public-notice requirements for school-district bidding; supports the procedural-incorporation claim. No conflict with the deliverables.

Stage-2 re-fetched live text: 'Notwithstanding Sections 20111 and 20112... without advertising for bids' authority for school-district cooperative/piggyback acquisitions; scope explicitly limited to data-processing equipment, materials, supplies, equipment, automotive vehicles, tractors, and other personal property. Services are mentioned only as incidental to the personal-property acquisition. Confirms Stage-1 / deliverable claim that §20118 does not extend §39803's services cap. Matches verbatim.

Stage-2 re-fetched live text: lease/lease-purchase authority for equipment/service systems including schoolbuses; no term limit imposed in this section (term limit comes from §39803). Matches Notes item (7) — §17450 et seq. is procedural-compliance scaffolding, not a term-cap modifier.

Two defects re-confirmed on Stage 2. First, the URL attached to this row in source-ledger.md line 95 is the Article 1 PORTAL link (codes_displayText.xhtml?...article=1.) rather than the §39807.5 pinpoint (codes_displaySection.xhtml?...sectionNum=39807.5.). Generic-portal-not-pinpoint hygiene issue; the pinpoint URL exists, loads, and was independently re-verified on 2026-06-24. Second, the pinpoint live text of §39807.5(e) reads: 'A charge under this section may not be made for the transportation of individuals with exceptional needs as defined in Section 56026.' §56026 (independently re-fetched on 2026-06-24) is a GENERAL definition of a special-education-eligible child (federal-IDEA-disability + needs special instruction beyond regular-program modifications + specified age + board-eligibility criteria) — it is NOT conditioned on the IEP including transportation as a related service. The Notes prose at research-findings.md line 101 ('pupils with disabilities WHOSE IEP INCLUDES TRANSPORTATION AS A RELATED SERVICE') therefore narrows the statutory scope. §39807.5 last amended Stats. 2022, Ch. 52 (AB 181), eff. 6/30/2022 — within currency.

Stage-2 re-fetched live text: applies to drivers 'providing school-related pupil transportation for compensation' employed or contracted by LEAs; exempts parents/relatives/guardians (§39875(c)(1)-(2)) and limited school-employee field-trip drivers up to 40 hours annually (§39875(d)). Added by Stats. 2023, Ch. 380 (SB 88), eff. 1/1/2024, operative 7/1/2025 per §39882. Substantive content matches Notes item (11). Ledger row says 'added by SB 88, Stats. 2023' without 'Ch. 380' — minor hygiene, not load-bearing.

Stage-2 spot-fetched independently to verify the paraphrase-drift defect on §39807.5(e). Live text confirms §56026 is a GENERAL definition of 'individual with exceptional needs' (federal-IDEA disability + need for special instruction beyond regular-program modifications + specified age category + board-eligibility criteria) and is not conditioned on transportation as a related service. Confirms Stage-1's paraphrase-drift finding.

citationVerifications.citation: California Legislative Information 2025-2026 bill search portal
citationVerifications.sourceSaysVsClaim: Stage-2 independently fetched Article 1 portal (codes_displayText.xhtml?...article=1.) and confirmed amendment-history table for §§39800-39809.5: every section last touched by 1999 Ch. 646 except §39800.1 (Stats. 2023 Ch. 48), §39800.5 (Stats. 2003 Ch. 559), §39803.5 (Stats. 2023 Ch. 445, eff. 1/1/2024), §39807.5 (Stats. 2022 Ch. 52). No post-2024 amendments to §§39800-39809.5. Current-law check holds at as-of 2026-06-23.

Stage-2 independently re-fetched on 2026-06-24 — the page LOADED with full content, confirming $119,100 services threshold effective 1/1/2026, derived from 3.772% Implicit Price Deflator adjustment under PCC §20111(a)/(e). Per the letter dated 12/8/2025, threshold rises from $114,800 to $119,100. Page loads cleanly — its inclusion on scratch/california.json sourcesUnreachable is in error (P2 cross-file inconsistency).

Stage-2 independently re-fetched on 2026-06-24 with one retry. URL 302-redirects to validate.perfdrive.com (Radware bot-protection wall under Claude-User user agent). Confirmed unreachable. Source-ledger.md line 94 records this page as 'Accessed 2026-06-23' WITHOUT an unreachable flag; scratch/california.json sourcesUnreachable does NOT list this page (it lists the Bid Threshold page instead, which actually loads). The unreachable flags are SWAPPED between these two pages — cross-file inconsistency. Substance (IEP-team determination of transportation as a related service) is cross-confirmable from §39807.5/§56026 plus 34 C.F.R. §300.34, so this page is not load-bearing.

mismatches: scratch/california.json sourcesUnreachable lists the CDE Bid Threshold 2026 page (cde.ca.gov/fg/ac/co/bidthreshold2025.asp) as bot-protection-redirected, but the page LOADS cleanly with full content (re-confirmed by Stage 2 on 2026-06-24). Conversely, the CDE Special Education Transportation Guidelines page (cde.ca.gov/sp/se/lr/trnsprtgdlns.asp) — recorded as accessed 2026-06-23 in source-ledger.md line 94 with NO unreachable flag — 302-redirects to validate.perfdrive.com (Radware bot wall) under Claude-User user agent (re-confirmed by Stage 2 on 2026-06-24). The unreachable-flag assignments are SWAPPED between these two pages across scratch/california.json and source-ledger.md.

Cal. Educ. Code §39807.5 row in source-ledger.md (line 95) attaches the Article 1 PORTAL URL (codes_displayText.xhtml?...article=1.) — NOT a §39807.5 pinpoint URL (codes_displaySection.xhtml?lawCode=EDC&sectionNum=39807.5.). Every other California §-row uses the pinpoint format — schema drift internal to the ledger.

scratch/california.json reports overall confidence 'High' while fieldVerdicts.districtVariance is 'unresolved'. The pack's substantive treatment (Field 5 = documented negative-result 'Unknown' rather than authoritative-conflict 'unresolved') is defensible under CLAUDE.md, but the High/Unresolved juxtaposition is not surfaced or qualified in research-findings.md or the JSON pack itself.

Research-findings.md Notes item (9) narrows §39807.5(e)'s 'individuals with exceptional needs as defined in Section 56026' to 'pupils with disabilities WHOSE IEP INCLUDES TRANSPORTATION AS A RELATED SERVICE.' Live §56026 (re-fetched 2026-06-24) is a GENERAL special-ed-eligibility definition, not conditioned on transportation as a related service. Paraphrase drift on a non-load-bearing fee-restriction note.

PCC §20111 source-ledger Currency cell mixes statutory amendment date ('Stats. 2017 Ch. 395') with administrative-indexed figure ('2026 services threshold $119,100'). The $119,100 figure is a CDE-issued §20111(e) annual adjustment, not a statutory amendment — typographically dense, risk of misreading.

summary: California's load-bearing legal claims (Max Term §39803(a) 5-year continuing-services cap with joint-renewal carry-forward including rate-increase provisions; §39803(b) 5-year lease/rental cap with 10-year annual-option-to-purchase/cancel variant; §39803.5 15/20-year zero-emission carve-out; Rebid §39802 transportation-specific $10,000 threshold with PCC §§20111-20112 procedural incorporation and 'other than lowest bidder' clause; Renewal §39803(a) joint-mutual + §39803(c) annual in-term renegotiation; full Statutory Citation chain) ALL re-verified verbatim on official leginfo on 2026-06-24, including independent re-fetch of §39800, §39802, §39803, §39803.5, PCC §20111, PCC §20112, PCC §20118, §17450, §39807.5, §39875, §56026, and the Article 1 portal. No post-2024 amendments to §§39800-39809.5 located in the live amendment history. The special-education differentiation finding (no separate procurement/term/rebid/renewal regime; §39807.5 parent-fee carve-out) holds. The District-Variance 'Unknown' is properly preserved as a documented negative result. No P1 defects. Stage 1's seven defects all reconfirmed on Stage 2 independent re-checks: (i) §39807.5 source-ledger URL is an Article 1 portal link not a §39807.5 pinpoint (P2 citation hygiene); (ii) the CDE SpEd Guidelines page is bot-protection-walled under the audit user agent and is recorded as accessed in the ledger AND missing from the JSON pack's sourcesUnreachable list, while the CDE Bid Threshold page (which loads cleanly) is erroneously ON that unreachable list — the flags are SWAPPED (P2 cross-file inconsistency); (iii) PCC §20111 ledger Currency cell conflates statutory amendment date with 2026 CDE-indexed $119,100 figure (P3); (iv) §39807.5 Notes prose narrows §56026's general 'individual with exceptional needs' definition to IEP-with-transportation, paraphrase drift on a non-load-bearing fee restriction (P3); (v) overall confidence 'High' is presented alongside an 'unresolved' District-Variance field without an explicit qualifier (P3); (vi) law-firm cross-confirmation sources listed in sourcesReVerified rather than a pathfinders section (P3); (vii) negative-search-path for District Variance does not mention FCMAT or State Controller K-12 Audit Guide (P3 — not required, optional tightening). Overall verdict: Minor. The audit pack itself is accurate and well-formed; no Stage-2 escalation, no Stage-2 dissolution.

issues.evidence: source-ledger.md line 95 attaches https://leginfo.legislature.ca.gov/faces/codes_displayText.xhtml?lawCode=EDC&division=3.&title=2.&part=23.5.&chapter=1.&article=1. to '§39807.5'. That URL is the Article 1 PORTAL (codes_displayText.xhtml — lists all sections 39800-39809.5), not a §39807.5 pinpoint (codes_displaySection.xhtml?lawCode=EDC&sectionNum=39807.5.). Generic-portal-not-pinpoint. Stage-2 independently confirmed the pinpoint URL exists and resolves to current §39807.5 text (last amended Stats. 2022, Ch. 52). Other California §-rows in the same ledger use the pinpoint pattern — internal schema drift.
issues.recommendedRemediation: Replace the URL in the §39807.5 source-ledger row with https://leginfo.legislature.ca.gov/faces/codes_displaySection.xhtml?lawCode=EDC&sectionNum=39807.5. for consistency with the other §-rows.

issues.evidence: Stage-2 independently re-fetched both pages on 2026-06-24 with one retry. The CDE Bid Threshold 2026 page (cde.ca.gov/fg/ac/co/bidthreshold2025.asp) — listed in scratch/california.json sourcesUnreachable as 'bot-protection redirect' — actually LOADS cleanly with full content confirming $119,100 effective 1/1/2026. The CDE Special Education Transportation Guidelines page (cde.ca.gov/sp/se/lr/trnsprtgdlns.asp) — recorded as accessed 2026-06-23 in source-ledger.md line 94 with no unreachable flag and NOT on the JSON pack's sourcesUnreachable list — actually 302-redirects to validate.perfdrive.com (Radware bot-protection wall) under the audit user agent. The flags are SWAPPED between the two pages across the JSON pack and the ledger.
issues.recommendedRemediation: Remove the Bid Threshold page from scratch/california.json sourcesUnreachable (it loads). Add the CDE SpEd Guidelines page to sourcesUnreachable, OR mark it 'partially verified — bot-protection wall under audit user agent; substantive guidance cross-confirmable via §39807.5(e), §56026, and 34 C.F.R. §300.34' in both the ledger row and the JSON pack.

issues.field: Special Education — Notes item (9) prose, research-findings.md line 101
issues.evidence: Notes prose: '§39807.5 prohibits charging transportation fees to pupils with disabilities WHOSE IEP INCLUDES TRANSPORTATION AS A RELATED SERVICE.' Live §39807.5(e) (re-fetched 2026-06-24): 'A charge under this section may not be made for the transportation of individuals with exceptional needs as defined in Section 56026.' §56026 (independently re-fetched 2026-06-24) is a GENERAL definition of a special-education-eligible child (IDEA disability + needs special instruction beyond regular-program modifications + specified age + board criteria) — not conditioned on the IEP including transportation as a related service. The deliverable paraphrase narrows the statutory scope on a non-load-bearing fee restriction.
issues.recommendedRemediation: Adjust the paraphrase to track §39807.5(e)/§56026: 'prohibits charging transportation fees to individuals with exceptional needs as defined in §56026.'

issues.evidence: scratch/california.json reports confidence 'High' while fieldVerdicts.districtVariance is 'unresolved'. CLAUDE.md guidance: 'Do not assign final High confidence unless ... the adversarial pass leaves no unresolved authoritative conflict.' The pack treats 'Unknown' as a documented negative result (no sourced, official counterweight surfaced — defensible under the same spec, which limits 'unresolved' to sourced credible counterweights). Still, the High/Unresolved juxtaposition is internally awkward and is not qualified anywhere in the deliverables.
issues.recommendedRemediation: Qualify the overall confidence as 'High on four substantive fields; District Variance preserved as documented negative-result Unknown,' or downgrade to 'Medium-High' to reflect the open Field 5.

issues.evidence: source-ledger.md line 90 Currency cell reads 'Last amended Stats. 2017 Ch. 395; 2026 services threshold $119,100.' The $119,100 figure is NOT a statutory amendment — it is the CDE-issued annual indexed adjustment under PCC §20111(e) per the 12/8/2025 Bid Threshold Letter (re-fetched 2026-06-24). Mixing the statutory amendment date and the indexed-figure date in the same Currency cell risks a reader misreading the statute as having been amended in 2026.
issues.recommendedRemediation: Split the Currency cell: keep 'Stats. amendment: 2017 Ch. 395 (SB 544)' in Currency, and move the '$119,100 effective 1/1/2026 (CDE Bid Threshold Letter per PCC §20111(e))' to a Notes/Relevant-Text column.

issues.evidence: scratch/california.json sourcesReVerified includes: '2026 PCC §20111 bid threshold ($119,100) cross-confirmed via SCLS, DWK, F3 Law, Lozano Smith, Liebert Cassidy Whitmore.' These are law-firm secondary summaries — proper pathfinders, not authority. The load-bearing authority IS official (CDE Bid Threshold Letter, Tier 4) and was re-fetched on Stage 2, so this is presentation hygiene only, not a tier-discipline breach in substance.
issues.recommendedRemediation: Move the law-firm cross-confirmation list out of sourcesReVerified and into a verification-notes 'pathfinders consulted' subsection, to keep authority-grade sources distinct from pathfinders.

issues.evidence: research-findings.md District Variance Exceptions entry (line 215) lists CDE bulletins, AG opinions, State Auditor, CSBA model policies BP 3540/BP 3311, and large-district samples (Alameda USD, SFUSD). It does NOT mention the California State Controller's K-12 Audit Guide or the Fiscal Crisis & Management Assistance Team (FCMAT), both of which regularly publish on district procurement issues. Coverage is reasonable on the watchdog axis; not exhaustive.
issues.recommendedRemediation: Optionally extend the negative search path note with FCMAT and State Controller K-12 Audit Guide checks — not required to resolve the field, but tightens the 'Unknown' justification.
